En: Think what you want, but, in your gut, you know otherwise.
Zh: 想想你要什么，但是你的直觉告诉你，你知道不是这么回事。

En: Either way, he looks at the bullet again, "this is enough to confront her with."
Zh: “不管怎么说，”他又看了一眼子弹，“这已经足够跟她当面对峙了。”

En: That's a big leap, but from what I've heard, this Ruby sounds pretty desperate. Either way, he looks at the bullet again, "this is enough to confront the washerwoman."
Zh: “这是个重大突破，但是根据我听到的消息，这位露比好像很绝望。不管怎么说，”他又看了一眼子弹，“这已经足够跟那位洗衣女工当面对峙了。”

En: That's something of a leap -- but we should be on our guard. Either way, he looks at the bullet again, "this is enough to confront the washerwoman."
Zh: “这是个重大突破——但是我们应该警惕些。不管怎么说，”他又看了一眼子弹，“这已经足够跟那位洗衣女工当面对峙了。”

En: Possibly.
Zh: “有可能。”

En: See anything interesting?
Zh: “发现什么有意思的东西了吗？”

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: The edge of a floorboard next to it looks scratched.
Zh: 旁边地板的边缘好像被划过。

En: What is it?
Zh: 是什么？

En: Look, a bullet! Not for a breechloader, though...
Zh: “看，一颗子弹！不过不是后膛枪的……”

En: Lieutenant, a bullet!
Zh: “警督，是一颗子弹！”

En: This bullet isn't for a breechloader like the murder weapon?
Zh: 这颗子弹不是凶器那种后膛枪用的？

En: A bullet, interesting.
Zh: 一颗子弹，有意思。

En: The lieutenant studies the bullet in your hand. "Still, interesting."
Zh: 警督仔细研究手中的子弹。“不过，还是很有意思。”

En: Yes, for muzzleloaders like the ones officers of the RCM carry. Interesting.
Zh: “是啊，RCM警官配的那种前膛枪用的。有意思。”

En: What do you think it's doing here?
Zh: “你觉得它为什么会在这里？”

En: Someone hid it as extra ammunition?
Zh: “有人藏起来，当做备用弹药？”

En: Ruby?
Zh: “露比？”

En: I don't know, could be. The washerwoman was clearly withholding something -- and she *does* rent this place out to people.
Zh: “不清楚，也许吧。那个洗衣女工肯定有所隐瞒——她*确实*把这个地方租给了别人。”

En: Let's go, then. [Leave.]
Zh: “那我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: The floorboard isn't interested -- maybe the Washerwoman is? You have enough to confront her with.
Zh: 这块地板不感兴趣——也许洗衣女工会呢？现在掌握的信息已经足够和她对峙了。

En: The floorboard doesn't care -- but maybe the Washerwoman does? You have enough to confront her with.
Zh: 这块地板没什么好关注的——也许应该去找那个洗衣女工？现在掌握的信息已经足够和她对峙了。

En: It's extra ammunition. She's locked and loaded, ready to fight some cops.
Zh: 这是备用弹药。她已经锁定目标，准备跟警察对着干了。

En: Holding the bullet, you get the feeling -- this isn't ammunition against you. It's for herself.
Zh: 握着手中的子弹，你有一种感觉——这枚子弹不是用来对付你的。是她留给自己的。

En: Seems to me like someone is armed and waiting for us.
Zh: 看来有人全副武装，就等着我们了。

En: Kim, I think this is extra ammunition against us.
Zh: “金，我觉得这些备用弹药是用来对付我们的。”

En: I think this is a last resort -- a bullet in case suicide becomes an option.
Zh: “我觉得这是最后一招——如果到了必要关头，这颗子弹可以用来自杀。”

En: Holding the bullet, you get the feeling -- this isn't ammunition against *you*. It's for herself.
Zh: 握着手中的子弹，你有一种感觉——这枚子弹不是用来对付*你*的。是她留给自己的。

En: Move the board aside.
Zh: 把木板移到一边。

En: As you look at the floorboards in this corner of the shack, it's clear one of them isn't quite level with the others.
Zh: 你看着棚屋角落的地板，很显然，其中有一块没有跟其它的对齐。

En: The hollow space underneath the floorboards is dark and damp. You can barely make out the mixture of sand and sawdust below.
Zh: 地板下的空洞黑暗而潮湿。你勉强辨认出下面是一滩沙子和锯末的混合物。

En: You stick your hand in and start combing through the sand. Dry, not like outside. Fine dust... and then -- something hard, wrapped in paper.
Zh: 你把手探进去，开始在沙子里面仔细搜寻。干燥，跟外面的不一样。细细的尘土……然后——你摸到一个包在纸里的硬东西。

En: You return the board to its original position.
Zh: 你把木板移回到原来的位置。

En: Search through the sand and sawdust.
Zh: 仔细翻遍沙子和锯末。

En: Oh, I don't want to stick my hand in there. [Leave.]
Zh: 呃，我不想把手伸进去。[离开。]

En: It's nothing. [Leave.]
Zh: 没什么有意思的。[离开。]

En: In time, dust will settle upon the wooden boards, covering up all tracks.
Zh: 灰尘及时地落在了木板上，掩盖了所有痕迹。

En: What's in here?
Zh: 里面是什么？

En: Nothing particular catches your eye. Looks like more reeds. There might be something hidden inside the sand, though.
Zh: 你的眼睛没发现什么特别的东西，看起来像是芦苇。不过，也许沙子里藏了些什么。

En: Something bad. Someone's night thoughts. A last resort. A bad idea.
Zh: 不好的东西。某人在深夜里的突发奇想。最后一招。一个坏主意。

En: A small cylindrical object. You pull it out -- a bullet.
Zh: 一个小型的柱状体。你把它取了出来——是一颗子弹。

En: A 9mm bullet to be exact. Fit for all muzzleloaders of that calibre, like your own Villiers pistol, for example.
Zh: 准确的说，是9毫米的子弹。所有那种口径的前膛枪都适用，打个比方：你的维利耶手枪就可以。

En: A 9mm bullet to be exact. Fit for all muzzleloaders of that calibre.
Zh: 准确的说，是9毫米的子弹，所有那种口径的前膛枪都适用。

En: On the table you see a bowl of water, a rough soap, and next to it a small hand mirror. A straight razor soaks inside the wash basin.
Zh: 桌上有一碗水，一块粗糙的肥皂，旁边还放着一支小手镜。洗手盆底部沉着一支刮胡刀。

En: Please attend to personal hygiene on your own time.
Zh: 有空闲的时候，记得注意个人卫生。

En: The lieutenant throws you an embarrassed glance as you consider the washbasin.
Zh: 当你考虑用洗手盆的时候，警督尴尬地看了你一眼。

En: Leave it for now.
Zh: 暂时先不管。

En: Thank god, I would have cut your throat.
Zh: 谢天谢地，我差点就割破你的喉咙了。

En: The centipede is exaggerating -- people don't actually cut their own throats when they're shaving. At least not accidentally.
Zh: 那只蜈蚣太夸张了——谁会在刮胡子的时候把喉咙割破啊。至少不可能是意外。

En: First wash the smell of death off yourself.
Zh: 先洗掉自己身上死亡的味道。

En: Wash the smell of death off yourself.
Zh: 洗掉自己身上死亡的味道。

En: Thank god you didn't go in there barehanded! Death smell is notoriously difficult to get off yourself and your clothes though and cleaning up takes a while...
Zh: 谢天谢地，你没有空手进去！众所周知，死亡的味道一旦沾上身，还有衣服，就要花很久才能清洗掉……

En: But man persists and after fifteen minutes pass, you look almost presentable. Sort of. The sudden absence of the morbid dead tissue smell instantly lifts your spirit.
Zh: 但是人类很能坚持，15分钟之后，你看起来可以见人了。差不多吧。突然没了病变坏死组织的味道，你的精神顿时振奋起来。

En: But man persists and finally your bruised hands look clean and presentable... sort of. The sudden absence of the morbid dead tissue smell is uplifting.
Zh: 但是人类很能坚持，最终你那双青肿的手变得整洁中看起来……勉强是吧。突然没了病变坏死组织的味道，让人感觉神清气爽。

En: Scrubbing all the necrotic tissue off your hands takes a while. The fingernails are especially hard. Death has made it's way into every nook and cranny...
Zh: 你花了好长一会儿才洗掉了手上的坏死组织。手指甲的部分是最艰难的。死亡渗入了每一条缝隙，每一处角落……

En: Should have used gloves. Detectives use gloves, when they go rummaging around in someone's dead brain...
Zh: 早知道应该用手套的。警探都会用手套，特别是在死人大脑里到处翻找的时候……

En: You don't have a face anymore. It's all baby-ass now.
Zh: 你已经没有脸了，现在全是婴儿屁股一样。

En: Yeah, purified yourself right back to infancy. You look like a baby!
Zh: 是啊，净化自己，一路回到婴儿时期。你看起来就像个婴儿！

En: Your hand shakes so hard you can't even muster up the courage to *try*. Maybe you're just too *wild* at heart?
Zh: 你的手抖地厉害，甚至无法鼓足*尝试*的勇气。也许是因为你的内心太过*狂野*？

En: *Was* shaving the right call?
Zh: 刮胡子*是*正确的决定吗？

En: Is shaving the right call?
Zh: 刮胡子是正确的决定吗？

En: On the table you see a bowl of water, a rough soap, and next to it a small hand mirror. A straight razor soaks inside the wash basin.
Zh: 桌上有一碗水，一块粗糙的肥皂，旁边还放着一支小手镜。洗手盆底部沉着一支刮胡刀。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The water reflects back a vague image of your face -- nose bulbous and red, hair unkempt, wrinkles lining the eyes and forehead. The 'stache is gigantic.
Zh: 水面映照出你模糊的轮廓——鼻子红彤彤的，像个圆球；头发蓬乱，眼角和前额爬满皱纹。还有一把巨大的胡子。

En: How would you know? You haven't even seen your face! Go take a peek in the mirror, then decide for yourself.
Zh: 你怎么知道？你甚至都没看见过自己那张脸！去镜子里瞄一眼，然后自己决定。

En: Time to shave. Get these mutton chops off.
Zh: 该刮胡子了。把这把络腮胡全剃掉吧。

En: Dresscode: True man.
Zh: 着装要求：真男人。

En: Like an artist with a brush or a master swordsman, you use the small mirror and the straight razor -- with some soap -- to remove all that unkempt hair from below the nose-line.
Zh: 像是手握画笔的艺术家，又或者是手握宝剑的剑神，你拿起小镜子和刮胡刀——沾了点肥皂——把鼻子下面那团乱七八糟的毛发刮了个干净。

En: Your hand trembles as you scratch at your cheek... oh no, that's not how a grown man shaves!
Zh: 刮到脸颊的时候，你的手微微颤抖……哦不，一个成年男人可不是这样刮胡子的。

En: They feel so smooth, surprisingly so. A feeling of freshness overcomes you, as if you just came from a cold bath.
Zh: 出人意料的是，它们感觉特别光滑。你感觉一阵神清气爽，就好像刚洗了个冷水澡。

En: This isn't sharp enough. Scrape harder.
Zh: 这个不够锋利，下手再重点。

En: Stop, for crying out loud! You're gonna cut your own throat.
Zh: 拜托，快住手！你快把自己的脖子划开了。

En: I *need* to shave the chops!
Zh: 我*需要*把胡子刮干净！

En: You'll be lookin' like a pansy without the chops! A fuckin' pansy!
Zh: 没胡子的话你跟那些基佬有什么区别！基佬懂吗！

En: A fresh start looms ahead. Clean yourself up and be born anew.
Zh: 一个新开始在你眼前隐隐若现。收拾好自己，重获新生。

En: The water reflects back a vague image of your clean-shaven face. Despite the bulbous nose, unkempt hair and persistent swelling, you look... a little younger, maybe?
Zh: 水面隐约映照出一张没有胡子的脸庞。虽然鼻子像圆球，头发乱蓬蓬，脸也一如既往的肿胀，你看起来……年轻了一些，也许吧？

En: You almost look like a professional.
Zh: 你差不多有点专业人士的样子了。

En: Your face, such as it is. A regular human face. Sans expression.
Zh: 你的脸，不过如此。一张普通人的脸。毫无表情。

En: The beardless nature of your cheeks makes The Expression seem even more like a terrifying grimace.
Zh: 你那没了胡须遮掩的面庞变得越发像一张恐怖的鬼脸。

En: There's no way to salvage this.
Zh: 已经无法挽救了。

En: Drop the razor in the water basin.
Zh: 把剃须刀扔进洗手盆。

En: Your hand refuses to move another millimetre. A small trickle of blood runs down your cheek.
Zh: 你的手不愿再移动分毫。一滴血珠慢慢从你的脸颊滑落。

En: The razor lands with a *clang*. A red trickle begins to spread in the water.
Zh: 剃须刀*叮当*一声落下了。一抹鲜红在水中扩散开来。

En: Feel your clean-shaven cheeks.
Zh: 摸摸没有胡子的面颊。

En: Hope this was the right decision.
Zh: 希望这是正确的决定。

En: Beneath the doubt sprouts a new vigour, like you've discarded some bad decisions. It feels as though you've passed through a ritual. Emerged from cold water, even...
Zh: 疑问之下，一股新的活力生根发芽，就像是抛弃了一些错误决定。你感觉自己好像经历了一场仪式。甚至是刚从冰水中浮了上来……

En: The sharp blade chafes against your skin, producing a scratching sound. The surface underneath the beard feels tender, the air brushing against it chilly.
Zh: 锋利的刀片划过你的皮肤，发出嘶啦的声响。胡须下面的皮肤摸起来很柔软，拂过面颊的空气略显冰凉。

En: The sharp blade chafes against your skin, producing a scratching sound. The surface underneath the beard feels tender, the air brushing against it chilly.
Zh: 锋利的刀片划破你的皮肤，发出嘶啦的声响。胡须下面的皮肤摸起来很柔软，拂过面颊的空气略显冰凉。
